walkthrough.title: Choose a Track
walkthrough.text: Let's get Heat: Pedal to the Metal set up. First, pick a track. The game comes with 2 double-sided game boards showing 4 different race tracks: USA, France, Great Britain, and Italy. For your first game, use the USA track. Each track shows the number of laps to race, the corners per lap, and the spaces per lap. For a learning game, consider racing just 1 lap instead of the standard 2 laps printed on the board. Place the chosen board in the middle of the table.
walkthrough.image_caption: Game board showing the USA track

walkthrough.title: Choose Colors and Take Components
walkthrough.text: Each player chooses a color and takes the matching race car, player mat, Gear pawn, and 12 Speed cards in their color. There are 6 player colors available. Each player also takes the 3 Starting Upgrade cards in their color. These Starting Upgrade cards have a value of 1, 2, or 3 and will be shuffled into your deck.
walkthrough.image_caption: Player mat with car, gear pawn, and speed cards

walkthrough.title: Check the Board for Heat and Stress Counts
walkthrough.text: Look at the game board for the track you chose. It tells you how many Heat cards and Stress cards each player must add to their car. Typically this is 6 Heat cards and 3 Stress cards per player, and that is the example used in the rulebook. Take the indicated number of Heat cards and Stress cards from the shared supply.
walkthrough.image_caption: Board showing Heat and Stress card counts

walkthrough.title: Set Up Your Player Mat
walkthrough.text: Place your player mat in front of you. It has three important spots: a left spot for your draw deck, a middle Engine spot for your Heat cards, and a play area where you will place cards each round. Take your 6 Heat cards and place them face up on the Engine spot in the middle of your player mat. These Heat cards start in your engine and will cycle through your deck as you race.
walkthrough.image_caption: Player mat with Engine spot holding 6 Heat cards

walkthrough.title: Build and Shuffle Your Draw Deck
walkthrough.text: Take your 12 Speed cards, 3 Starting Upgrade cards, and 3 Stress cards. Shuffle all 18 of these cards together thoroughly. Place this shuffled deck face down on the left spot of your player mat. This is your draw deck for the race.
walkthrough.image_caption: Shuffled draw deck placed on player mat

walkthrough.title: Set Your Starting Gear
walkthrough.text: Place your Gear pawn on the 1st gear position on your player mat. Everyone starts the race in 1st gear. During the game you will shift gears up and down to control how many cards you play each round.
walkthrough.image_caption: Gear pawn set to 1st gear

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Stress Card Reserve
walkthrough.text: Place the remaining Stress cards face up in a shared pile close to the board. This is the Stress card reserve. Players will take Stress cards from this pile when they spin out during the race. There are 37 Stress cards total in the game.
walkthrough.image_caption: Stress card reserve pile near the board

walkthrough.title: Place Cars on the Starting Grid
walkthrough.text: Randomly determine a starting order for all participating cars. Place them one at a time on the starting grid, filling the spots in ascending order. The starting grid is printed on the board near the start/finish line. Each space on the track has spots closer to or farther from the Race Line. The spot closest to the Race Line is the better position.
walkthrough.image_caption: Cars placed on the starting grid

walkthrough.title: Draw Your Starting Hand
walkthrough.text: Each player draws 7 cards from their draw deck into their hand. These are the cards available to you for the first round of the race. Keep your hand hidden from other players. You are now ready to begin the Grand Prix!
walkthrough.image_caption: Hand of 7 cards drawn from the deck

summary.title: Draw Your Starting Hand

Keep hand hidden from other players
Ready to race!

walkthrough.title: Round Structure Overview
walkthrough.text: Heat is played over several rounds. Each round, ALL players complete 4 mandatory steps: shift gears, play cards, move their car, and draw back up to 7 cards. Steps 1 and 2 happen simultaneously for all players. Then steps 3 through 9 happen one player at a time, starting with the frontmost car (closest to the Race Line) and working backward through the field. The race continues until all cars have completed the required number of laps.
walkthrough.image_caption: Round overview showing all 9 steps

walkthrough.title: Step 1: Shift Gears
walkthrough.text: At the start of each round, check which gear your car is currently in. You may leave it in the same gear, or shift it one position up or down. The gears range from 1st to 4th. Your gear determines how many cards you play this round: 1st gear means 1 card, 2nd gear means 2 cards, 3rd gear means 3 cards, and 4th gear means 4 cards. There is a special option: you can shift two positions up or down in one step, but doing so costs 1 Heat from your Engine immediately. Everyone shifts gears at the same time.
walkthrough.image_caption: Gear pawn positions from 1st to 4th gear

walkthrough.title: Step 2: Play Cards
walkthrough.text: Your current gear tells you exactly how many cards to play from your hand. In 1st gear, play 1 card. In 2nd gear, play 2 cards. In 3rd gear, play 3 cards. In 4th gear, play 4 cards. Place the cards face down in your Play Area on the player mat. Do not reveal them yet. Once all players have finished steps 1 and 2, proceed to step 3 one player at a time, starting with the car in the lead.
walkthrough.image_caption: Cards placed face down in the Play Area

walkthrough.title: Step 3: Reveal and Move
walkthrough.text: Flip the cards in your Play Area face up to reveal their values. Add all the values together to determine your total Speed. Move your car forward exactly that many spaces on the track. Always place your car on the Spot closest to the Race Line if it is available. Cars never block you from passing through a space, even if the track looks crowded. However, if you end your move in a space where all spots are occupied, you must stop in the first space behind that has a free spot, as close to the Race Line as possible. If two cars are side by side, the one closest to the Race Line is considered ahead.
walkthrough.image_caption: Cards revealed, speed totaled, car moved on track

walkthrough.title: How Stress Cards Work
walkthrough.text: All players start with 3 Stress cards shuffled into their deck. Stress cards represent lapses in concentration. When you play a Stress card, you do not know its speed in advance. You flip cards from the top of your draw deck until you find a Speed card. That Speed card replaces the Stress card and is added to your played cards. Any non-Speed cards flipped along the way go immediately to your discard pile. This means a Stress card gives you a random speed between 1 and 4. Resolve each played Stress card before moving.
walkthrough.image_caption: Stress card resolution: flip until a Speed card appears

walkthrough.title: Step 4: Adrenaline
walkthrough.text: This step only applies to the last car to move each round. In races with 5 or more cars, the last 2 cars get Adrenaline. If you qualify, you may add 1 extra speed to your move, which means moving 1 additional space. You also gain access to 1 extra Cooldown, usable during the React step. Adrenaline cannot be saved for future rounds. It is a catch-up mechanic designed to help trailing cars.
walkthrough.image_caption: Last-place car receives Adrenaline boost

walkthrough.title: Step 5: React (Cooldown and Boost)
walkthrough.text: In this step you activate symbols you have access to, in any order you want. The two main symbols in the core game are Cooldown and Boost. Cooldown lets you take Heat cards from your hand and put them back on your Engine, clearing them out of your deck cycle. You gain Cooldown from 1st gear (Cooldown 3, meaning move up to 3 Heat) and 2nd gear (Cooldown 1). Boost lets you pay 1 Heat from your Engine to flip cards from your draw deck until you find a Speed card, then move your car by that amount. Boost increases your Speed for the Check Corner step. You may only Boost once per turn.
walkthrough.image_caption: Cooldown and Boost symbols on the gear track

walkthrough.title: Step 6: Slipstream
walkthrough.text: Slipstreaming is optional and only available if you end your move next to another car or in a space directly behind a car. If you choose to Slipstream, move your car 2 spaces forward. This represents drafting behind another car at high speed. Important: Slipstreaming does NOT increase your Speed value for the Check Corner step. It is purely extra movement after your speed has been calculated.
walkthrough.image_caption: Car slipstreaming 2 spaces behind another car

walkthrough.title: Step 7: Check Corner
walkthrough.text: This step only applies if you crossed through a corner this round. Each corner has a Speed Limit printed on the track next to a Corner Line. If your total Speed for the round is equal to or lower than the Speed Limit, you are fine and nothing happens. If your Speed exceeds the Speed Limit, you must pay Heat equal to the difference. For example, if the Speed Limit is 5 and your total Speed was 8, you pay 3 Heat by moving 3 Heat cards from your Engine to your discard pile. If you drive through multiple corners in one turn, pay for each one separately, starting with the first Corner Line you crossed.
walkthrough.image_caption: Corner with Speed Limit and Corner Line marked

walkthrough.title: Spinning Out
walkthrough.text: If you do not have enough Heat in your Engine to pay for exceeding a corner's Speed Limit, you pay all the Heat you have and then spin out. Three things happen immediately. First, your car moves back to the first available space before the corner that caused the spin out. Second, you take extra Stress cards into your hand: 1 Stress card if you are in 1st or 2nd gear, or 2 Stress cards if you are in 3rd or 4th gear. Third, your Gear pawn moves to 1st gear. Spinning out is devastating, so manage your Heat carefully.
walkthrough.image_caption: Car spun out, moved back before the corner

walkthrough.title: Heat Cards Explained
walkthrough.text: Heat cards are the core resource and risk system. Your 6 Heat cards start on the Engine spot. Whenever you pay Heat, you move a Heat card from the Engine to your discard pile. Later it gets shuffled into your draw deck and eventually reaches your hand. The problem is that Heat cards in your hand cannot be played or discarded. They are dead weight that reduces your effective hand size. The only way to get Heat cards out of your hand is to Cooldown, which moves them from your hand back to the Engine. This creates a cycle: spend Heat to go fast, then slow down in low gears to Cooldown and recover the Heat.
walkthrough.image_caption: Heat card cycle: Engine to discard to deck to hand to Engine

walkthrough.title: Cluttered Hand
walkthrough.text: In rare situations, you can have so many Heat cards in your hand that you do not have enough playable cards for your current gear. If this happens, play as many playable cards as you can and cover the remaining card slots with Heat cards from your hand. Your car does not move this turn. Instead, move your Gear pawn to 1st gear, place all cards from your Play Area into your discard pile, and skip directly to step 9: Replenish Hand.
walkthrough.image_caption: Hand full of Heat cards with no playable cards

walkthrough.title: Step 8: Discard
walkthrough.text: This is an optional step. After resolving your movement and corners, you may discard any cards from your hand that you do not want to keep for future rounds. Place discarded cards face up onto your discard pile. However, you can never choose to discard Stress or Heat cards. Nobody can browse through discard piles; only the top card is public knowledge.
walkthrough.image_caption: Discarding unwanted cards from hand

walkthrough.title: Step 9: Replenish Hand
walkthrough.text: This is a mandatory step. First, take all cards from your Play Area and put them onto your discard pile. Then draw cards from your draw deck until you have 7 cards in hand. If your draw deck runs out at any point during the game, immediately shuffle your discard pile to form a new draw deck. However, if this happens mid-turn, do not shuffle cards currently in your Play Area back in, as they have not been discarded yet. The next player then proceeds with their steps 3 through 9.
walkthrough.image_caption: Cards moved from Play Area to discard, hand refilled to 7

walkthrough.title: How to Win
walkthrough.text: You win by being the first player to cross the finish line after the number of laps indicated on the board. If two or more cars finish in the same round, the car furthest ahead wins, with the spot closest to the Race Line as the tiebreaker. Once you cross the Finish Line on the last lap, several special rules apply: Slipstreaming is not allowed after crossing or across the Finish Line. Disregard any speed limits in corners past the Finish Line. Remove finished cars from the track and place them in order on the Hall of Fame space. Continue playing until all cars finish. Adrenaline is based on the number of cars that started the race, not the remaining cars.
walkthrough.image_caption: Cars crossing the finish line

summary.title: Round Structure Overview
bullets: 4 mandatory steps per round: Shift Gears, Play Cards, Move, Replenish Hand
Steps 1-2 are simultaneous; Steps 3-9 resolve one player at a time (lead car first)
Race continues until all cars complete the required laps

bullets: Play cards face down equal to your gear number (1st=1, 2nd=2, 3rd=3, 4th=4)

If destination space is full, stop at first available spot behind

Speed card replaces the Stress card (random value 1-4)
Non-Speed cards flipped along the way go to discard pile
Resolve each Stress card before moving

bullets: Only for last car (or last 2 cars in races with 5+ cars)
+1 extra speed and +1 extra Cooldown
Cannot be saved for future rounds

summary.title: Step 5: React (Cooldown and Boost)
bullets: Cooldown: move Heat from hand back to Engine (1st gear = 3, 2nd gear = 1)
Boost: pay 1 Heat, flip deck until Speed card found, move that many spaces
Boost counts toward Check Corner speed; only once per turn
Activate symbols in any order

summary.title: Step 6: Slipstream
bullets: Optional: if you end next to or behind another car, move 2 spaces forward
Does NOT count toward Speed for Check Corner
Not allowed across the Finish Line on the final lap

summary.title: Step 7: Check Corner
bullets: Only if you crossed a Corner Line this round
Speed <= Speed Limit: safe, nothing happens
Speed > Speed Limit: pay Heat = difference (e.g., Speed 8 - Limit 5 = 3 Heat)
Multiple corners: pay each separately, first corner first

summary.title: Spinning Out
bullets: Triggered when you cannot pay enough Heat for a corner
Pay all remaining Heat, then: move car back before the corner
Take 1 Stress (1st/2nd gear) or 2 Stress (3rd/4th gear) into hand
Gear pawn moves to 1st gear

summary.title: Heat Cards Explained
bullets: 6 Heat cards start on Engine; paying Heat sends them to discard pile
Heat cards eventually cycle into your hand as dead weight
Cannot play or discard Heat cards from hand
Cooldown (low gears) moves Heat from hand back to Engine

summary.title: Cluttered Hand
bullets: If too many Heat cards in hand, not enough playable cards for your gear
Play what you can, fill remaining slots with Heat cards
Car does not move; Gear pawn goes to 1st gear
Skip to Step 9: Replenish Hand

summary.title: Step 8: Discard
bullets: Optional: discard unwanted cards from hand to discard pile
Cannot discard Stress or Heat cards
Only top card of discard pile is public

summary.title: Step 9: Replenish Hand
bullets: Move all Play Area cards to discard pile
Draw back up to 7 cards in hand
If draw deck empty, shuffle discard pile into new draw deck
Do not shuffle Play Area cards back in mid-turn

summary.title: How to Win
bullets: First car to cross Finish Line after required laps wins
Tiebreaker: car furthest ahead, then closest to Race Line
No Slipstreaming across Finish Line; ignore corner limits after Finish Line
Continue until all cars finish; Adrenaline based on original car count

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Grand Prix
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first round with 4 players racing on the USA track. Player 1 is Red, Player 2 is Blue, Player 3 is Green, and Player 4 is Yellow. Everyone has drawn 7 cards, their Gear pawns are in 1st gear, and their 6 Heat cards are on the Engine. The cars are lined up on the starting grid. Let's race!
walkthrough.image_caption: Four cars on the USA starting grid

walkthrough.title: Round 1: Everyone Shifts Gears
walkthrough.text: All players start in 1st gear. In 1st gear you only play 1 card, which is slow but safe. Red shifts up to 2nd gear to play 2 cards this round. Blue shifts up to 3rd gear, wanting to burst out of the gate. Green stays in 1st gear, playing it cautious. Yellow shifts up to 2nd gear. Remember, all players shift gears simultaneously before anyone plays cards.

walkthrough.title: Everyone Plays Cards
walkthrough.text: Now each player places cards face down in their Play Area based on their gear. Red (2nd gear) plays 2 cards face down. Blue (3rd gear) plays 3 cards face down. Green (1st gear) plays just 1 card face down. Yellow (2nd gear) plays 2 cards face down. Nobody reveals their cards yet.

walkthrough.title: Red Reveals and Moves
walkthrough.text: Since the starting grid is random, let's say Red is in the lead position. Red flips their 2 cards: a Speed 4 and a Speed 3, totaling 7. Red moves their car 7 spaces forward on the track. There is a corner ahead with a Speed Limit of 5, but Red did not cross the Corner Line, so no check is needed. Red ends their move on the best available spot on a straight section. Nice safe start.
walkthrough.image_caption: Red moves 7 spaces on the straight

walkthrough.title: Blue Goes Fast — Too Fast
walkthrough.text: Blue is next. Blue flips 3 cards: Speed 4, Speed 3, and Speed 2, totaling 9. Blue rockets forward 9 spaces. But Blue crosses the Corner Line of a corner with a Speed Limit of 5. Blue's Speed of 9 exceeds the limit by 4, so Blue must pay 4 Heat from the Engine. Blue moves 4 Heat cards from the Engine to the discard pile. Blue now only has 2 Heat left in the Engine. That was aggressive!
walkthrough.image_caption: Blue blows through the corner, paying 4 Heat

walkthrough.title: Why That Was Risky
walkthrough.text: Blue spent 4 of 6 Heat cards on a single corner in the very first round. Those 4 Heat cards will cycle through Blue's discard pile into the deck, then into Blue's hand, where they become dead weight you cannot play or discard. Blue will eventually need to shift down to low gears and use Cooldown to recover them. On a 2-lap race, burning that much Heat in round 1 can leave you helpless later. Sometimes fast is too fast.

walkthrough.title: Green Plays It Safe with Cooldown
walkthrough.text: Green stayed in 1st gear, so Green plays just 1 card: a Speed 3. Green moves 3 spaces forward. Not exciting, but here is the key: 1st gear gives you Cooldown 3, meaning Green could move up to 3 Heat cards from hand back to Engine. Green does not have any Heat in hand yet, so the Cooldown does not help this round. But Green's cautious approach preserves all 6 Heat for when they really need it.
walkthrough.image_caption: Green moves 3 spaces, Heat fully intact

walkthrough.title: Yellow Reveals a Stress Card
walkthrough.text: Yellow is in 2nd gear and flips 2 cards. One is a Speed 4, but the other is a Stress card. To resolve the Stress card, Yellow flips cards from the top of their draw deck. The first flip is a Heat card, which goes to the discard pile. The second flip is a Speed 2, which is a Speed card. That Speed 2 replaces the Stress card. Yellow's total is 4 plus 2, equaling 6. Yellow moves 6 spaces. The Stress card gave a random result, but it worked out okay.
walkthrough.image_caption: Yellow resolves a Stress card by flipping from the deck

walkthrough.title: Yellow Tries to Slipstream
walkthrough.text: Yellow ends their move in the space directly behind Red's car. Slipstreaming is available! Yellow chooses to Slipstream and moves 2 additional spaces forward, pulling ahead of Red. Remember, this extra movement does NOT count toward the Speed Limit check for corners. Slipstreaming is a powerful way to gain free distance by drafting behind other racers.
walkthrough.image_caption: Yellow slipstreams past Red

walkthrough.title: Blue Makes a Mistake: Boosting After a Big Corner
walkthrough.text: Let's rewind and say Blue also had the option to Boost during the React step. Blue decides to Boost, paying 1 more Heat from the Engine. Blue flips cards until finding a Speed 3. Blue moves 3 more spaces. But wait, Blue just paid the 5th Heat card. Only 1 Heat remains in the Engine. Blue's Speed for the Corner check is now even higher because Boost adds to it. If another corner comes next round, Blue could easily spin out with almost no Heat left to pay.
walkthrough.image_caption: Blue boosts recklessly with nearly empty Engine

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Managing Your Heat Engine
walkthrough.text: Blue's mistake is one of the most common errors new players make. They treat Heat as expendable fuel and burn through it early. But Heat has a double penalty: spending it leaves you vulnerable at corners, and when those spent Heat cards reach your hand, they clog it up. A good rule of thumb for beginners: try to keep at least 3 Heat in your Engine at all times during the first lap. Think of Heat as a safety buffer, not something to spend freely.
walkthrough.image_caption: Blue's nearly empty Engine

walkthrough.title: Blue's Second Mistake: Wrong Gear for the Corner
walkthrough.text: Next round, Blue is approaching another corner. Blue is still in 3rd gear, which means playing 3 cards. Even with all low-value cards, 3 cards will likely add up to more than the upcoming Speed Limit of 4. Blue should have downshifted to 2nd or even 1st gear before this corner. Instead, Blue plays 3 cards totaling 7 against a Speed Limit of 4. That is 3 more Heat to pay, but Blue only has 1 Heat in the Engine. Blue spins out!
walkthrough.image_caption: Blue spins out at the corner

walkthrough.title: The Spin Out
walkthrough.text: Blue pays the 1 remaining Heat but still owes 2 more that cannot be paid. Blue spins out. Three things happen: Blue's car is moved back to the first available space before the corner. Blue takes 2 Stress cards into hand because Blue was in 3rd gear. Blue's Gear pawn moves to 1st gear. Blue went from leading the pack to last place in one devastating moment. The extra Stress cards will also make future hands less predictable.
walkthrough.image_caption: Blue's car moved back, picking up Stress cards

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Read the Track Ahead
walkthrough.text: Blue's second mistake was not planning ahead. Always look at the corners coming up and ask yourself: can I afford to go through at this speed? The track shows the Speed Limit for each corner and the number of spaces until the next corner. Use this information to decide your gear. If a tight corner is 3-4 spaces away, start downshifting early. You cannot drop from 4th to 1st gear in one round without paying Heat for the double shift.
walkthrough.image_caption: Track showing Speed Limits and corner distances

walkthrough.title: Green Uses Cooldown Wisely
walkthrough.text: A few rounds in, Green has picked up 2 Heat cards in hand from earlier corner payments. Green downshifts to 1st gear, plays just 1 card, and uses the Cooldown 3 from 1st gear to move both Heat cards from hand back to the Engine. Green now has a clean hand of playable cards again and a full Engine. This is the Heat cycle working perfectly: spend, recover, spend again.
walkthrough.image_caption: Green uses Cooldown to restore Heat to Engine

walkthrough.title: Red Picks the Right Gear for Each Section
walkthrough.text: Red has been shifting between 2nd and 3rd gear on straights, then dropping to 2nd gear before corners. This balanced approach means Red has spent only 2 Heat after several rounds while staying competitive in position. Red is not the fastest on any single straight, but Red is consistently in the top half of the pack without any crisis moments. Consistency beats aggression in Heat.
walkthrough.image_caption: Red steadily navigating the track in mid-pack

walkthrough.title: Everyone Replenishes
walkthrough.text: At the end of each player's turn, played cards go to the discard pile and each player draws back up to 7 cards. This is when you might draw those Heat cards that were cycling through your deck. If you see Heat cards in your new hand, plan to Cooldown soon, or at least avoid going into high gear where you need to play many cards. A hand with 3 Heat cards and only 4 playable cards means you can only safely play 4th gear if all 4 of those cards are worth playing.
walkthrough.image_caption: Drawing back up to 7 cards after playing

walkthrough.title: The Final Straight
walkthrough.text: As cars approach the final corner before the Finish Line, this is the moment of truth. Red and Yellow are neck and neck. Both shift up to 4th gear for maximum speed on the final straight. Red plays 4 cards totaling 14 and crosses the Finish Line. Yellow plays 4 cards totaling 12 and also crosses. But Red is further ahead, so Red wins the race. Green finishes third with a steady approach. Blue recovers from the spin out to limp across in fourth.
walkthrough.image_caption: Red crosses the Finish Line first

walkthrough.title: Congratulations!
walkthrough.text: You have seen the full arc of a Heat race: the careful opening, the risk of pushing too hard through corners, the devastating spin out, the power of Slipstreaming, and the sprint to the finish. Blue learned the hard way that managing your Heat Engine is the key to this game. Green showed that patience and Cooldown keep you in the race. Red demonstrated that reading the track and picking the right gear wins races.

walkthrough.title: Before You Race
walkthrough.text:
Before you start your own Grand Prix, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for beginner strategy and pacing advice.

Second, reference the Appendix for quick definitions of every game term and card type.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Start your engines!

summary.title: Welcome to the Grand Prix
bullets: Scripted tutorial with 4 players (Red, Blue, Green, Yellow) on the USA track
All start in 1st gear with 6 Heat on Engine and 7 cards in hand
Follow along even with fewer players

summary.title: Round 1: Everyone Shifts Gears
bullets: Red shifts to 2nd gear, Blue to 3rd, Green stays in 1st, Yellow shifts to 2nd
Gear = number of cards played this round
All players shift simultaneously

summary.title: Everyone Plays Cards
bullets: Each player places cards face down equal to their gear number
No revealing yet — cards stay hidden until the Reveal step
Steps 1-2 complete; now resolve one player at a time

summary.title: Red Reveals and Moves
bullets: Red: Speed 4 + Speed 3 = 7 total, moves 7 spaces
No corner crossed, so no Speed Limit check needed
Safe start on a straight section

summary.title: Blue Goes Fast — Too Fast
bullets: Blue: Speed 4 + 3 + 2 = 9, crosses corner with Speed Limit 5
Pays 4 Heat (9 - 5 = 4) from Engine to discard pile
Only 2 Heat remaining in Engine after round 1

summary.title: Why That Was Risky
bullets: 4 of 6 Heat spent in round 1 leaves Blue vulnerable
Spent Heat cycles into hand as dead weight cards
Blue will need low gears and Cooldown to recover

summary.title: Green Plays It Safe with Cooldown
bullets: Green in 1st gear: plays 1 card (Speed 3), moves 3 spaces
1st gear grants Cooldown 3 (not needed yet, no Heat in hand)
All 6 Heat preserved for when they count

summary.title: Yellow Reveals a Stress Card
bullets: Yellow flips: Speed 4 + Stress card
Stress resolution: flip Heat card (discard), then Speed 2 (replaces Stress)
Total speed: 4 + 2 = 6, moves 6 spaces

summary.title: Yellow Tries to Slipstream
bullets: Yellow ends behind Red — Slipstream available
Moves 2 extra spaces, pulling ahead of Red
Slipstream does NOT count toward corner Speed check

summary.title: Blue Makes a Mistake: Boosting After a Big Corner
bullets: Blue Boosts (pays 1 Heat), flips Speed 3, moves 3 more spaces
Now only 1 Heat left in Engine — extremely vulnerable
Boost adds to Speed for corner checks, making it even riskier

summary.title: Lesson: Managing Your Heat Engine
bullets: Spending Heat early leaves you vulnerable at future corners
Spent Heat clogs your hand later as unplayable dead weight
Beginner tip: keep at least 3 Heat in Engine during first lap

summary.title: Blue's Second Mistake: Wrong Gear for the Corner
bullets: Blue stays in 3rd gear approaching a Speed Limit 4 corner
3 cards total 7 vs limit of 4 = 3 Heat owed, but only 1 in Engine
Should have downshifted to 1st or 2nd gear before the corner

summary.title: The Spin Out
bullets: Blue cannot pay: spins out
Car moved back before corner, takes 2 Stress cards (3rd gear penalty)
Gear pawn reset to 1st gear — from first to last

summary.title: Lesson: Read the Track Ahead
bullets: Always check upcoming corners and their Speed Limits
Start downshifting early — cannot drop from 4th to 1st in one round
Use the track's printed distances to plan gear changes

summary.title: Green Uses Cooldown Wisely
bullets: Green downshifts to 1st gear, uses Cooldown 3
Moves 2 Heat from hand back to Engine
Clean hand + full Engine = ready for the next section

summary.title: Red Picks the Right Gear for Each Section
bullets: Red alternates 2nd/3rd gear on straights, drops to 2nd before corners
Only 2 Heat spent after several rounds — consistent and competitive
Consistency beats aggression in Heat

summary.title: Everyone Replenishes
bullets: Played cards go to discard, draw back up to 7
Heat cards in hand reduce your effective playable cards
Plan Cooldown when you see Heat accumulating in hand

summary.title: The Final Straight
bullets: Red and Yellow go 4th gear on the final straight
Red: 14 speed, crosses Finish Line first — wins!
Yellow finishes 2nd, Green 3rd, Blue 4th after the spin out

summary.title: Congratulations!
bullets: Blue learned: manage Heat, don't burn it all in round 1
Green showed: patience and Cooldown keep you competitive
Red demonstrated: read the track, pick the right gear, win the race

summary.title: Before You Race
bullets: Check out General Tips for beginner strategy
Reference the Appendix for game terms and card types
Ask your personal GM Guide anytime — tap Q&A during play

walkthrough.title: Respect the Speed Limits
walkthrough.text: The single most important rule for new players: respect the Speed Limits on corners. Every corner has a posted limit, and exceeding it costs precious Heat from your Engine. The math is straightforward: if you go through a corner at Speed 8 with a limit of 5, you pay 3 Heat. But the real cost is much higher because those 3 Heat cards will cycle into your hand as dead weight later. Before you play cards, look at where you will end up and count whether you will cross a Corner Line. If so, plan your cards to stay at or near the Speed Limit.

walkthrough.title: Gear Down Before Corners, Gear Up on Straights
walkthrough.text: The core rhythm of Heat is shifting gears to match the track. On long straights, shift up to 3rd or 4th gear to play more cards and cover more ground. As a corner approaches, shift down to 2nd or even 1st gear. Remember, you can only shift 1 gear per round for free. If you are in 4th gear and a corner is 2 rounds away, you need to start shifting down now. Shifting 2 gears at once costs 1 Heat, which is sometimes worth it in emergencies, but not as a regular habit.

walkthrough.title: Keep a Heat Reserve
walkthrough.text: Beginners often spend Heat freely in the first few rounds and regret it later. A good starting rule: keep at least 3 Heat in your Engine at all times during the first lap. This gives you a safety net for unexpected corners or last-second moves. On the second lap you can be more aggressive because you are closer to the Finish Line and have fewer corners to navigate. Think of your Heat as a bank account. You would not spend your entire savings on the first day of a vacation.

walkthrough.title: Use Cooldown Strategically
walkthrough.text: Cooldown is the only way to recover Heat from your hand back to the Engine. First gear gives Cooldown 3, and 2nd gear gives Cooldown 1. If you have 2 or 3 Heat cards clogging your hand, plan a round in 1st gear to clear them all at once. The best time to Cooldown is when you are approaching a section of the track with tight corners anyway, since low gear and low speed go together naturally. Do not wait until your hand is completely clogged to start cooling down.

walkthrough.title: Slipstream Whenever You Can
walkthrough.text: Slipstreaming is free speed. If you end your move next to or behind another car, you can move 2 extra spaces at no cost. The best part: Slipstream movement does not count toward your Speed for corner checks. This means you can go through a corner at exactly the Speed Limit, then Slipstream 2 extra spaces past it for free. Experienced players position themselves to slipstream off opponents regularly. When choosing which cards to play, consider whether landing behind someone sets up a Slipstream.

walkthrough.title: Manage Your Stress Cards
walkthrough.text: You start with 3 Stress cards in your deck, and spinning out gives you more. Stress cards are not terrible because they resolve as a random Speed card between 1 and 4. But the randomness can be dangerous near corners where you need precise speed control. Try to play Stress cards on long straights where any random speed is fine. Avoid playing them right before corners where going even 1 speed over the limit would cost you Heat.

walkthrough.title: Count Your Cards
walkthrough.text: As you play, keep a rough count of what is left in your deck. Your deck has 18 cards total (12 Speed + 3 Starting Upgrades + 3 Stress) plus any Heat cards that have cycled in. If you have already played your high-value Speed cards, your remaining draws will be lower-value, making it safer to go into higher gear near corners. Conversely, if you have not seen your Speed 4 cards yet, be cautious because they might show up from a Stress card resolution at the worst time.

walkthrough.title: Do Not Ignore the Discard Step
walkthrough.text: After your move and corner checks, you have the option to discard cards from your hand. New players often skip this step, but it is valuable. If you are holding low-value Speed cards that you do not want to draw again next round, discard them. This thins your deck and improves the quality of your future draws. Remember: you cannot discard Heat or Stress cards, only Speed and Upgrade cards. Use the discard step to shape your deck for the section of track ahead.

Those spent Heat cards cycle into your hand as dead weight later
Before playing cards, check if you will cross a Corner Line

walkthrough.title: Heat Economy Mastery
walkthrough.text: Advanced Heat play starts with treating your 6 Heat cards as a renewable resource, not a consumable one. The Heat cycle is: Engine, discard pile, draw deck, hand, and then Cooldown back to Engine. Efficient racers spend 2-3 Heat on a corner, immediately plan a Cooldown round 1-2 turns later, and cycle those same Heat cards again. The key insight is that you want Heat constantly rotating through this cycle rather than sitting idle on the Engine or stuck in hand. A Heat card used twice during a race is twice as valuable as one that sits on the Engine unused.

walkthrough.title: Optimal Gear Sequencing
walkthrough.text: The best racers plan their gear sequence 2-3 rounds ahead by reading the track layout. A typical sequence might be: 3rd gear on the straight, 2nd gear approaching the corner, 1st gear through the corner with Cooldown, then back up to 2nd and 3rd on the next straight. The critical skill is knowing exactly when to start downshifting. Count the spaces to the next corner and figure out how many rounds of movement it will take to reach it. If you are 12 spaces away in 3rd gear averaging 8 speed, that is about 1.5 rounds, meaning you need to shift down this round.

walkthrough.title: Corner Speed Optimization
walkthrough.text: Going exactly at the Speed Limit through a corner is ideal but rarely possible. Advanced players aim to exceed the Speed Limit by 1-2 to maintain pace, deliberately paying 1-2 Heat as a calculated investment. The math is simple: paying 2 Heat to go 2 extra spaces now is often worth it if those 2 spaces put you in Slipstream range or keep you ahead of a competitor. The danger zone is exceeding by 3 or more, especially with fewer than 3 Heat in Engine. Always know your exact Heat count before deciding your speed through a corner.

walkthrough.title: Slipstream Positioning
walkthrough.text: Expert players think about Slipstreaming several steps ahead. The ideal scenario is going through a corner at exactly the Speed Limit so you pay zero Heat, then landing next to or behind another car to Slipstream 2 spaces for free, effectively getting 2 extra spaces that do not count against the corner's Speed Limit check. To set this up, you need to control your speed precisely. Sometimes playing a lower-value card to land in Slipstream range is better than playing a high card that overshoots into empty track.

walkthrough.title: Boost Timing
walkthrough.text: Boosting costs 1 Heat and gives a random Speed 1-4 by flipping from your deck. The crucial detail is that Boost speed counts toward corner checks. This makes Boosting before a corner extremely risky because you cannot control the result. The optimal time to Boost is on a long straight where any extra speed is beneficial, or right after clearing the last corner before the Finish Line on the final lap. Boosting also helps when you need to reach Slipstream range and your played cards fell 1-2 short.

walkthrough.title: Deck Management
walkthrough.text: Your deck is not random — it is a resource you can shape. There are three tools: the Discard step lets you throw away unwanted Speed cards to thin your deck. The Speed values you choose to play dictate what remains in the deck for future draws. And the Stress card resolution flips cards from your deck, effectively cycling through it faster. Advanced players use the Discard step aggressively on straights, throwing away Speed 1 and Speed 2 cards they do not need, so future draws are higher quality. When approaching corners, keep low-value cards for controlled speed.

walkthrough.title: Positional Racing
walkthrough.text: Position on the track matters more than raw speed. Being directly behind another car gives you Slipstream access. Being in last gives you Adrenaline. Being just ahead of a corner means you can take a Cooldown round while others are still approaching. The turn order each round goes front to back, which means leading cars must commit to their speed before trailing cars see what happens. Trailing cars can react to leaders. Sometimes deliberately staying 1-2 spaces behind the leader to maintain Slipstream access is better than pushing into the lead where you lose that free speed.

walkthrough.title: The Two-Gear Shift
walkthrough.text: Shifting 2 gears in one round costs 1 Heat but can be game-winning in specific situations. The most powerful use is jumping from 2nd gear to 4th gear on a long straight when you are trailing and need to make up ground. The second-best use is emergency braking from 3rd to 1st gear when you miscalculated a corner approach. The cost of 1 Heat for a 2-gear shift is almost always worth it when it saves you from spinning out, which costs far more than 1 Heat. Plan double shifts as deliberate tactical moves, not panic reactions.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Sprint Strategy
walkthrough.text: The final stretch of the last lap changes the calculus completely. Speed limits after the Finish Line are ignored, and Slipstreaming across the Finish Line is not allowed. This means the last straight before the finish is pure speed with no catches. Save at least 2-3 Heat for this section so you can Boost and still have Heat to pay for the final corner. Shift to 4th gear for the sprint, play your highest-value cards, and Boost if needed. The ideal endgame is clearing the last corner with Heat to spare, then going all out on the final straight.

walkthrough.title: Reading Your Opponents
walkthrough.text: In a tight race, reading your opponents is crucial. Watch their Engine: how many Heat cards do they have left? If an opponent has 1 Heat in Engine and is approaching a corner in 3rd gear, they are about to spin out or be forced to crawl. Watch their Gear pawn: if someone in 4th gear is 3 spaces from a corner, they are in trouble. Watch their discard pile top card for information about what they have played. Use this information to decide whether to race for position or let them crash and pass them after. The best move is sometimes patience.

entries.definition: The core cards in each player's deck. Each player has 12 Speed cards in their color with values ranging from 1 to 4. Speed cards are played from hand to determine how far your car moves. There are 3 copies each of values 1, 2, 3, and 4 per player (72 total across all 6 players).

Cards that start on your Engine and represent the thermal energy of your race car. You spend Heat by moving cards from Engine to discard pile to pay for exceeding corner Speed Limits, Boosting, or shifting 2 gears. Heat cards that reach your hand are dead weight: they cannot be played or discarded. The only way to return them to the Engine is Cooldown. Each player typically uses 6 Heat cards (48 total in the box).

Cards that represent lapses in concentration. Each player starts with 3 shuffled into their draw deck (37 total in the box). When played, a Stress card is resolved by flipping cards from the draw deck until a Speed card appears, giving a random speed between 1 and 4. Additional Stress cards are gained by spinning out. Stress cards cannot be voluntarily discarded from hand.

Three special cards each player receives at setup, with values 1, 2, and 3. They are shuffled into your starting draw deck along with Speed and Stress cards. Starting Upgrade cards function like Speed cards when played. There are 18 total across all 6 players.

Your personal face-down deck of cards on the left spot of your player mat. You draw from this deck to replenish your hand to 7 cards. When the draw deck is empty, shuffle your discard pile to form a new draw deck. Do not shuffle Play Area cards back in mid-turn.

Your personal pile of used cards. Played cards go here at the end of your turn. Only the top card is public information. When your draw deck runs out, your discard pile is shuffled to become a new draw deck.

The space on your player mat where you place cards face down during Step 2 (Play Cards) and flip them face up during Step 3 (Reveal & Move). Cards in the Play Area are moved to the discard pile during Step 9 (Replenish Hand).

Each player's personal board with spots for the draw deck (left), Engine (middle with 6 Heat cards), and Play Area. Also shows the gear track from 1st to 4th gear with associated Cooldown values. There are 6 player mats in player colors.
A small pawn placed on your player mat to track your current gear (1st through 4th). Moved during the Shift Gears step. Each player has 1 Gear pawn in their color.
Your car miniature placed on the track to show your position. Available in 6 player colors. Cars never block passage through spaces but can block spots within a space.

2 double-sided boards providing 4 tracks: USA, France, Great Britain, and Italy. Each track shows corners with Speed Limits, the number of laps, starting grid positions, the Hall of Fame space, and the Finish Line.

The middle spot on your player mat where Heat cards are stored face up. Heat is paid by moving cards from the Engine to the discard pile. Heat is recovered by Cooldown, which moves Heat cards from your hand back to the Engine. You start with 6 Heat on the Engine (or as indicated by the chosen track).

Your current gear determines how many cards you must play each round. 1st gear = 1 card, 2nd gear = 2 cards, 3rd gear = 3 cards, 4th gear = 4 cards. Higher gears give more speed but less control. Lower gears provide Cooldown abilities: 1st gear has Cooldown 3, 2nd gear has Cooldown 1.

The total value of all your played cards for the round, including any resolved Stress cards and Boost. This determines how many spaces your car moves. Speed is also checked against corner Speed Limits.

A number printed next to each corner on the track. If your total Speed exceeds this value when you cross the Corner Line, you must pay Heat equal to the difference. Exceeding with insufficient Heat causes a spin out.

A curved section of the track marked by a Corner Line and a Speed Limit. When you cross a Corner Line during your move, you must check your Speed against the limit. Each track has a specific number of corners per lap, shown on the board.

A line drawn across the track at the entrance to a corner. If your car's path crosses this line during a round, you must check your Speed against the corner's Speed Limit. Multiple Corner Lines can be crossed in a single turn.

A section of the track that can hold one or more cars. Each space has a number of Spots. Cars move through spaces to advance around the track. The number on the track between corners indicates how many spaces until the next corner.

An individual position within a space where a single car can be placed. Spaces have multiple spots arranged in a row. The spot closest to the Race Line is the premium position and determines who is ahead when two cars share a space.

The inner line of the track. The spot closest to the Race Line is the preferred position in each space. When two cars occupy the same space, the one closer to the Race Line is considered ahead for all purposes including turn order and finishing position.

The section of the track before the Finish Line where cars are placed at the beginning of the race. Cars are placed randomly, filling spots in ascending order. The starting grid determines the initial turn order for round 1.

The line on the track that cars must cross on the final lap to complete the race. Special rules apply after crossing: no Slipstreaming allowed, corner Speed Limits are ignored. Cars that finish are placed on the Hall of Fame space.
A space on the game board where finished cars are placed in the order they crossed the Finish Line. This determines the final race standings.
An additional line on the track used only in Advanced Play with the Legends Module (not in the core game). Legends cars use this line for their automated movement.

Step 1 of each round. All players simultaneously decide whether to shift their Gear pawn up 1, down 1, or keep it the same. Shifting 2 positions is allowed but costs 1 Heat. This is the first decision each round and dictates how many cards you will play.

Step 2 of each round. Place cards from your hand face down into your Play Area. The number of cards equals your current gear (1st = 1, 2nd = 2, 3rd = 3, 4th = 4). All players complete this simultaneously.

Step 3. Flip your played cards face up and add their values to determine Speed. Move your car forward that many spaces. Resolve any Stress cards before moving. Players resolve this step one at a time from front to back.
Step 4. A catch-up mechanic for the last-place car (or last 2 cars in 5+ player races). Grants +1 speed (move 1 extra space) and +1 Cooldown during the React step. Cannot be saved for future rounds.
Step 5. Activate Cooldown and/or Boost symbols in any order. Access to symbols comes from your current gear, Adrenaline, or cards played (in advanced play via the Garage Module).

An ability gained from low gears (1st gear = Cooldown 3, 2nd gear = Cooldown 1) and sometimes from Adrenaline. Lets you move Heat cards from your hand back to the Engine, clearing dead weight and restoring your Heat supply. The number indicates the maximum Heat cards you can move.

An ability that costs 1 Heat from your Engine. You flip cards from your draw deck until a Speed card appears, then move your car that many additional spaces. Non-Speed cards flipped are discarded. Boost speed counts toward corner Speed checks. You may Boost at most once per turn.

Step 6. Optional free movement of 2 spaces. Available when you end your move next to or directly behind another car. Does NOT count toward Speed for corner checks. Not allowed across the Finish Line on the last lap. A key positioning tactic for advanced play.

Step 7. Mandatory check whenever you crossed a Corner Line this round. Compare your total Speed (including Boost, but not Slipstream) to the corner's Speed Limit. If over, pay Heat equal to the difference. Multiple corners crossed in one turn are checked separately, first corner first.

Occurs when you cannot pay enough Heat for exceeding a corner's Speed Limit. You pay all remaining Heat, then: (1) car moves back to first available space before the corner, (2) take 1 Stress card into hand if in 1st/2nd gear or 2 Stress cards if in 3rd/4th gear, (3) Gear pawn moves to 1st gear. Devastating setback.

Step 8. Optional step where you may discard Speed and Upgrade cards from your hand. You cannot discard Heat or Stress cards. Useful for deck thinning and shaping future draws. Only top card of discard pile is public.

Step 9. Mandatory final step of your turn. Move all cards from Play Area to discard pile, then draw cards until you have 7 in hand. If your draw deck is empty, shuffle your discard pile into a new draw deck first.

Moving a Heat card from the Engine to the discard pile. Required when: exceeding a corner Speed Limit (pay the difference), Boosting (pay 1), or shifting 2 gear positions (pay 1). If you cannot pay required Heat at a corner, you spin out.

A rare situation where Heat cards in your hand leave you without enough playable cards for your current gear. Play as many playable cards as possible, cover the rest with Heat from hand. Car does not move. Gear pawn moves to 1st gear. Skip to Step 9.
One complete circuit of the track. The number of laps per race is printed on the board for each track (typically 2 for the USA track). The race ends when all cars complete the required laps.

In Steps 3-9, players resolve one at a time starting with the frontmost car (closest to the Race Line) and proceeding backward through the field. The leading car commits first, giving trailing cars a reaction advantage.

If experienced drivers are playing with rookies, the experienced players can remove 1 or 2 Heat cards from their own car to level the playing field. This reduces their Heat buffer and makes corners more punishing.
The shared face-up pile of extra Stress cards placed near the board during setup. Players take Stress cards from this reserve when they spin out.
An advanced play module described in the second rulebook. Provides upgraded cards that grant additional symbols (like Cooldown or Boost) when played. Not used in the core game.
An advanced play module that adds automated AI opponents to the race. These legendary drivers use the Legends Line on the track and follow their own movement rules. Not used in the core game.
An advanced play format described in the second rulebook where players race across multiple tracks in a series, accumulating points across races to determine an overall champion.
